Average Volatility Trend
Each point is a season's average. The scale is open-ended — the axis can keep climbing as regulations evolve.

How To Read These Numbers

Volatility score = total lap inversions ÷ (grid size × total laps). It normalises for different race lengths and grid sizes so seasons can be fairly compared. A score above 1.0 means on average, every driver swapped places with at least one other driver relative to the starting grid, every single lap. The 2026 regulations were designed to push this number higher.

Made by Kritagya Loomba GitHub LinkedIn

"Formula 1", "F1", and related marks are trademarks of Formula One Licensing BV. Race data sourced via the FastF1 library for non-commercial, educational purposes.
